contentBlocks.original:
ॐ मौनव्याख्या प्रकटितपरब्रह्मतत्वंयुवानं
वर्शिष्ठान्तेवसदृषिगणैरावृतं ब्रह्मनिष्ठैः |
आचार्येन्द्रं करकलित चिन्मुद्रमानन्दमूर्तिं
स्वात्मरामं मुदितवदनं दक्षिणामूर्तिमीडे ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ maunavyākhyā prakaṭita parabrahmatatvaṃ yuvānaṃ
varśiṣṭhānte vasad ṛṣigaṇair āvṛtaṃ brahmaniṣṭhaiḥ |
ācāryendraṃ karakalita cinmudram ānandamūrtiṃ
svātmarāmaṃ muditavadanaṃ dakṣiṇāmūrtimīḍe ‖
contentBlocks.translation: I salute Sri Dakshinamurti, who is not subject to time, who makes known the truth of Brahman through the implied meaning of words, who is surrounded by disciples who are themselves Rishis and committed to the knowledge of Brahman, who is the teacher of teachers, whose hand is held in the gesture of widom, whose nature is fullness, who revels in himself, and who has a smiling face.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; मौनव्याख्या (maunavyākhyā) – silent explanation (mauna:silence | vyākhyā:explanation); प्रकटित (prakaṭita) – revealed; परब्रह्मतत्वं (parabrahmatattvaṃ) – essence of supreme Brahman (para:supreme | brahma:Brahman | tattvaṃ:essence); युवानं (yuvānaṃ) – youthful; वर्शिष्ठान्ते (varśiṣṭhānte) – among the best (varśiṣṭha:best | ante:among); वसद् (vasad) – dwelling; ऋषिगणैः (ṛṣigaṇaiḥ) – by groups of sages (ṛṣi:sage | gaṇaiḥ:by groups); आवृतं (āvṛtaṃ) – surrounded; ब्रह्मनिष्ठैः (brahmaniṣṭhaiḥ) – committed to Brahman (brahma:Brahman | niṣṭhaiḥ:committed to); आचार्येन्द्रं (ācāryendraṃ) – teacher of teachers (ācārya:teacher | indraṃ:of teachers); करकलित (karakalita) – hand-held (kara:hand | kalita:held); चिन्मुद्रं (cinmudraṃ) – gesture of wisdom (cit:wisdom | mudraṃ:gesture); आनन्दमूर्तिं (ānandamūrtiṃ) – form of bliss (ānanda:bliss | mūrtiṃ:form); स्वात्मरामं (svātmarāmaṃ) – reveling in self (sva:self | ātmarāmaṃ:reveling); मुदितवदनं (muditavadanaṃ) – with a smiling face (mudita:smiling | vadanaṃ:face); दक्षिणामूर्तिम् (dakṣiṇāmūrtim) – Dakshinamurti; ईडे (īḍe) – I praise;
